Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Native American subject vertical file, [ca.1850-2000.]
Collection Number: 9205

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Native American subject vertical file, [ca.1850-2000.]
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
9205
Abstract:
This collection contains ephemera covering a broad variety of topics relating to Native Americans.
Creator:
Huntington Free Library
Huntington Free Library
Quanitities:
129 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

This collection contains ephemera covering a broad variety of topics. Brochures, correspondence, programs from events, theses manuscripts, monographs, maps, music, photographs, plays, postcards, reprints, publications, small books, and government bills and statements, are included organized by subject. Collection was established as a catch all for items pertaining to Indians that were collected by staff of the Huntington Free Library. Collection contains a vast range of reference material, that covers anthropology, archaeology, culture, education, politics, and many other Native American topics. Collection also provides history on various locations and cities. Many unique, author signed and annotated, and privately printed items. In some cases the collection contains information about the aquisition of objects that now may reside at the National Museum of the American Indian.
NOTES

Only a fraction of the actual subject headings are given in the record; see the guide for the complete listing.
Forms part of the Native American Collection. (NAC)
See also the Native American artist vertical file, #9241, Native American biography vertical file, #9242, and Native American publications vertical file, #9246.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Native American subject vertical file, #9205. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Cibola, Seven Cities of (including Hawikuh) 3
Scope and Contents
Marco da Nizza, A relation of the reverend father Frier Marco de Nica / touching his discovery of the Kingdome of Ceuola or Cibola, situate about 30 degrees of latitude to the north of Nueua Espanna, 1539, an extract of a letter of captaine Francis Vasques de Coronado, written to a Secretary of the right noble Don Anotonio de Mendoza typescript.

Conferences and Meetings
Box 132
Conferences and Meetings - Oversize
Scope and Contents
An account of conferences held, and treaties made, between Major-General Sir William Johnson, Bart., and the chief sachems and warriours of the Mohawks, Oneidas, Onondagas ... Indian nations in North America, at their meetings on different occasions at Fort Johnson in the county of Albany, in the colony of New York, in the years 1755 and 1756. Photostats.
